summary: Derive 0035's render-side `extent` predicate (lines|characters, each+whole grain) and retire `max_lines` into it — the shipped line budgets re-spell as extent over the same selections (0035 amended)

new.description: The new predicate's fixture home (one per rule, `tests/rust.md`): each-grain (a member/embedded value whose rendered extent exceeds the bound fires; within-bound passes) and whole-grain (a selection's summed rendered extent over the bound fires) at both units (lines, characters). Render-side: a member with a resolved reference/include whose projection exceeds the bound fires even though its source body does not — the axis 0035 rejected `max_lines` for. Plus the retirement's loud edge: a committed lock still carrying the retired `max_lines` predicate refuses at load (the closed vocabulary's ordinary unknown-predicate reject, contract.md 'clause').

edit.description: Add `extent` to the closed vocabulary and RETIRE `max_lines` (0035 amended: extent subsumes it). New `Predicate::Extent` carrying its declared unit (lines | characters) and bound; its parse arm in the label->predicate table (where `max_lines` sat, ~462) — an unknown unit rejected at load is the unit's admissibility — and its label/field arms (label ~571, field ~625/639/669/682). Remove `Predicate::MaxLines` entirely: the variant + doc (~247-248), parse arm (~462), label arm (~571), field arms (~639/682). `extent` is a **node-scope** predicate like `max_len`: it names no field and measures the selected item's own rendered size.

Evaluate `extent` at both grains render-side and remove the `max_lines` eval. Each-grain (per selected item's rendered extent <= bound) and whole-grain (the selection's summed rendered extent <= bound — the ambient-context budget the grain axis gives for free, 0035). Measurement is render-side — the bytes the item contributes to its projection as emit resolved them, decidable off the committed artifacts the gate holds — NOT `features.body_lines` (that was `max_lines`'s source-side count). A file member's extent is its projection; an embedded value's is its rendered span in the host artifact (the load-bearing case — centercode's posture kinds are embedded). Remove `Predicate::MaxLines` (eval ~895/896, the fixed-answer doc ~256, whole-grain arm ~219) and migrate/drop its inline test fixtures (~1547, ~1910, ~2077, ~2315, ~2368). NOTE for build: `features.body_lines` and its `LineCount` layout primitive (kind.rs:1260/1328) may orphan once their sole predicate consumer is gone — confirm on disk; 0035 names no `LineCount` retirement, so do not invent one.

The `ClauseRow` struct (~2704) gains the optional `unit` column (snake_case wire key, `#[derive(Deserialize)]`-read), lifted into `Predicate::Extent` at load; remove the `max_lines` reconstruction arm with the variant, and update the bound-doc comments naming `max_lines` (~2691, ~2778, ~2823/2831) to `extent`. Two-sided by construction (`sdk.md`, 'The engine seam').

The committed built-in lock re-derives once the shipped defaults re-spell: its four `max_lines` clause rows — `command.max_lines` (~300), `memory.max_lines` (~496), `rule.max_lines` (~831), `skill.max_lines` (~966) — become `extent` rows (unit=lines, same bounds). Regenerated data (see the file header), never hand-edited; `tests/builtin_lock_frozen.rs` byte-compares the re-emission and fails loud if it drifts. (`src/builtin_lock.rs` is the pure loader and carries no `max_lines` — untouched.)

Add an `extent(unit, bound)` constructor where `maxLines` sat (~131), unit typed `'lines' | 'characters'`, returning `{ key: 'extent', ... }`; REMOVE the `maxLines` constructor — extent subsumes it. Update the clause-key doc list (~25) that enumerates `required`/`max_len`/`max_lines`.

Re-spell the shipped `maxLines` adoptions as extent budgets over the SAME selections carrying the EXISTING bounds (0035 amended: the subsumption carries the existing cited budgets across, no NEW budget opinion enters): `skillDefaultContract`'s `maxLines(500)` (~1138) -> `extent('lines', 500)`, `rule` `maxLines(200)` (~1272) and `memory` `maxLines(200)` (~1313) -> `extent('lines', 200)`, each keeping its advisory severity and cite, its guidance reworded to name render-side measurement. `commandDefaultContract` reuses `skillDefaultContract` by `.filter(...)` (~1191), so re-spelling the skill clause flips both `skill` and `command` lock rows — three source edits, four lock rows. Drop the `maxLines` import (~26).

`clauseRow` (~64) lowers `extent`: its bound rides `nodeScopeBoundArgs` (~89/~119) — swap the retired `max_lines` key in the guard there (~120) for `extent`, drop `max_lines` from the doc lists (~58/~116) — and its `unit` needs a new optional column emitted onto the `ClauseRow`. (ADMISSION-JOINS-FILE-TEMPLATE shipped at 631bc83 and no longer contends this file — its declarations.ts change was to `templatesFor`, not clause lowering.)
Swap the `maxLines` re-export (~38) for `extent` — the root barrel's constructor surface follows the vocabulary change.
Regenerated TS mirror of the `ClauseRow` struct once its `unit` column lands — the generated seam, not hand-authored; run the SDK generate step and commit the diff.

SDK-side: the `extent` constructor produces the expected clause value and its declaration row carries the unit + bound columns; a round-trip proves the wire key matches the Rust reader. Drop the `maxLines` coverage with the constructor.
Migrate the `maxLines` uses to `extent`: the import (~30), the `maxLines(300)` clause fixture (~180), and the `"max_lines"` predicate-key assertion (~218) -> `extent('lines', 300)` / `"extent"`.
The shipped-default assertions naming `max_lines` re-point to `extent`: the predicate-key list (~86, ~117) and the memory-contract clause-key assertion (~132).

Default-contract test: the shipped clause's label flips `*.max_lines` -> `*.extent` and its diagnostic reworded render-side; fixtures asserting on it re-point and any snapshot regenerates. Mechanical migration following the label, not new behavior.
Same label flip `memory.max_lines` -> `memory.extent` in the memory default-contract fixtures; re-point assertions, regenerate snapshots. Mechanical.
The memory-gate fixtures asserting on the `max_lines` clause label/diagnostic re-point to `extent`. Mechanical migration.
Contract-template fixtures naming `max_lines` re-point to `extent`. Mechanical migration following the label.
Lock-declaration-row fixtures asserting the emitted `max_lines` clause rows re-point to `extent` rows (with the new `unit` column). Mechanical.
Dial-kind fixtures dialing a clause addressed `*.max_lines` re-point to `*.extent`. Mechanical migration following the label.
Reporter fixtures rendering a `max_lines` finding re-point to the `extent` label/diagnostic. Mechanical.
Acceptance fixtures naming `max_lines` re-point to `extent`; the associated snapshot regenerates (see the snapshot edit below). Mechanical migration.
The `insta` snapshot carrying a `max_lines` diagnostic line regenerates to the `extent` label/wording once the shipped clause re-spells. Regenerated fixture, `cargo insta accept`.
CLI fixtures naming `max_lines` (a finding or explain line) re-point to `extent`. Mechanical.
Fail-loud fixtures referencing the `max_lines` clause re-point to `extent`. Mechanical migration following the label.
Session-start reporter fixtures naming `max_lines` re-point to `extent`. Mechanical.

The predicate-exhaustive match (~173) listing `Predicate::MaxLines { .. }` follows the vocabulary: drop the `MaxLines` arm, add `Extent { .. }`. This match is the schema oracle's completeness pin, so it must track the enum.
The closed-vocabulary fixture constructing `Predicate::MaxLines { max: 9 }` (~213) re-points to `Predicate::Extent { .. }`. Mechanical migration following the enum.

tests.asserts: each-grain and whole-grain `extent` fire on rendered-extent overruns at both units (lines, characters) and pass within bound; render-side measurement catches an overrun a source-side count would miss; a lock still carrying the retired `max_lines` predicate refuses at load
the predicate-exhaustive oracle tracks the enum: `Extent` present, `MaxLines` gone

acceptance: `extent(unit, bound)` is a closed-vocabulary predicate: each-grain bounds a selected item's rendered extent, whole-grain bounds the selection's summed rendered extent, unit is lines or characters, measurement is render-side off committed artifacts, and an unknown unit is refused at load (contract.md, 'clause': the predicate vocabulary is closed, an unknown predicate is rejected at load). `max_lines` is gone from the vocabulary and a lock carrying it refuses at load; the three shipped line budgets (skill/command 500, rule 200, memory 200) re-spell as `extent('lines', N)` over the same selections with no new budget opinion, and the built-in lock re-derives to match. `pnpm --dir sdk test` + `cargo test` green.
notes: Routes 0035's amendment (725188e): extent SUBSUMES max_lines; the 3 cited budgets carry across, no NEW opinion. command reuses skillDefaultContract (builtins.ts:1191), so 3 source edits flip 4 lock rows. body_lines + its LineCount primitive may orphan once max_lines' eval goes — confirm at build, don't invent its retirement (0035 names none). ADMISSION shipped (631bc83): the blockedBy is stale, unblocked to open. scoped at b804e98.

summary: Ship `settings-local` (0036): `.claude/settings.local.json`, a json-document at the local commitment class — read in place, gated, never emitted; documented keys typed, residue opaque

new.description: The new kind's fixture home (one per shipped kind, `tests/rust.md`), modeled on `tests/plugin_manifest_kind.rs` (json-document file member) crossed with `tests/dial_kind.rs` (local locus). Fixtures at the real layout `.claude/settings.local.json` (never an invented path): the kind owns the file as a `json-document` whose top-level keys are its fields; identity is the fixed singleton path (`unitShape: file`, stem `settings.local`, no identity field); the discovery override finds the always-gitignored document and `check` derives its rows AT READ TIME while emit writes nothing at its path (the local-locus contract, dial precedent — `tests/local_locus.rs` is the generic-machinery home and needs no edit); the documented-key clauses fire on their violations and the unschematized residue passes opaque; and `check` announces the active member (`src/check.rs` `Announcement.local_members`). Each format fact cites the live settings docs.

edit.description: Add the `settingsLocal` kind + a `SettingsLocal` interface + `settingsLocalDefaultContract`, modeled on `pluginManifest` (~593) with the local commitment lifted from `sdk/src/dial.ts`:70. Kind: `locus: { kind: 'at', root: '.claude', glob: 'settings.local.json', commitment: 'local' }`, `format: 'json-document'`, `unitShape: 'file'` (singleton identity from the fixed stem — no `identityField`), `registration: []`. The `SettingsLocal` interface types the DOCUMENTED top-level keys and names the unschematized residue opaque (the plugin-manifest interface pattern). `settingsLocalDefaultContract` carries the documented-profile clauses the LIVE settings docs settle — FETCH code.claude.com/docs/en/settings and cite+date each clause (external-fact bar); near-empty is admissible if the format documents little decidable schema (the installed-plugin / almost-empty-contract precedent, builtins.md 'Default contracts'). Fields, not members (0036): a locally-registered hook or enablement stays opaque residue named as such, never a modeled member.

Export `settingsLocal`, `settingsLocalDefaultContract`, and the `SettingsLocal` type from the provider face (type block ~11-24, value block ~25-48), following the `pluginManifest` export — a `.claude/` artifact rides the claude-code subpath, never dial's root export.

The engine's read-side def so `check` can extract the local document. Add `claude_code_settings_local()` modeled on `claude_code_plugin_manifest()` (~323) — `Format::JsonDocument`, `UnitShape::File`, the documented-key `Extraction` (opaque residue named as such) — with `.local()` appended as `temper_dial()` does (~405), and register it in `all_kinds()` (~412). `src/kind.rs` carries `Commitment::Local` + `Format::JsonDocument` already — no edit there.
The pure loader's two hard-coded kind-name lists gain `settings-local`: the sorted roster assertion (~52-68) and the clause-kind match (~100-113). No loader logic changes — data-driven over the lock.

Re-derives (regenerated data, never hand-edited rows — see the file header): a new `[[declaration.kind]]` block (`commitment = 'local'`, `format = 'json-document'`) plus its default-contract `[[declaration.clause]]` rows. Separately, the file's HAND-AUTHORED header prose updates by hand: the kind enumeration and counts (~13-26), and the two now-falsified 'the one kind' claims — `dial` as 'the one kind ... declaring a `local` commitment class' (~33-36) and `plugin-manifest` as 'the one kind ... at the `json-document` format' (~50-60) — each now names a second.

The re-derivation seam: `MEMBERLESS_BUILTIN_PROGRAM` binds `settings-local` via `expect` — add the import (~31-55) and its `{ kind: settingsLocal, clauses: settingsLocalDefaultContract }` entry (~57-72). This is what re-emits the rows `src/builtin_lock.toml` embeds; run `pnpm --dir sdk build` then `cargo test --test builtin_lock_frozen` to exercise the cross-language seam (`sdk.md`, 'The engine seam').
Extend the emitted-declaration-row fixtures to carry the new `settings-local` kind fact + its default-contract clause rows. Mechanical, following the roster.

Add `settingsLocal` / `settingsLocalDefaultContract` to the audited import + array (~19-45) so the 'well-formed clause array' and 'non-empty cite' sweeps cover it (~49-66), plus a per-kind assertion that `settings-local` is a local-locus json-document kind at `.claude/settings.local.json` (following the `mcpServer`/`marketplace` per-kind assertions ~136).

tests.asserts: `settings-local` owns `.claude/settings.local.json` as a json-document at the local commitment class; the discovery override finds the gitignored document and `check` derives its rows at read time; emit writes nothing at its path; the documented-key clauses fire and the opaque residue passes; the active member is announced
the embedded `src/builtin_lock.toml` byte-matches the SDK re-emission once `settings-local` joins the memberless program — the frozen-lock seam holds with the new kind fact + default-contract rows

acceptance: `settings-local` is a shipped kind: `.claude/settings.local.json` read as a json-document at the local commitment class — discovered under the ignore override, gated in place, never an emit input or target, its rows derived at read time and its presence announced (builtins.md, 'The shipped kinds'; 0036). Documented keys are typed with cited clauses; a locally-registered hook or enablement stays opaque residue named as such, never a modeled member. The built-in lock re-derives to carry the new kind + default contract and the frozen-lock seam holds. `pnpm --dir sdk test` + `cargo test` green.
notes: Routes 0036 (63e1f22): its sole Consequences bullet — kind, default contract, fixture under the discovery override — is this one atomic entry. (settings-local-kind) fork already absent from open-questions; no deletion owed. blockedBy EXTENT: both regenerate builtin_lock.toml. No per-key settings schema exists today — the default contract is authored fresh from the live settings docs, cited. src/{builtin,contract,kind}.rs unchanged, data-driven. scoped at 4cc3081.

summary: Derive 0037's verifier-type resolution: retype `verified_by` into a species union — script (path) + telemetry (documented event names), each resolved at admissibility; probe deferred

per.section: requirement — a shipped kind, not a primitive

edit.description: Retype the verifier slot. `Requirement.verifiedBy?: string` (~284) becomes `verifier?: Verifier`, a discriminated union with two active species this slice — add `Verifier` plus the `script(path: string)` and `telemetry(events: readonly string[])` constructors (both erase to `{ species, ... }`). PROBE IS NOT TYPED HERE: 0037 keeps probe a documented pattern until its transcript surface is documented or a second consumer types it — do not mint a `probe()` constructor. The `telemetry` constructor documents its admissible event names (InstructionsLoaded, Skill, UserPromptExpansion, PostToolUse — code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks, retrieved 2026-07-17 per 0037; build re-verifies against live docs at encode). Reword the interface doc (~277) `verifiedBy wires the behavioral remainder` to the typed verifier. Overlaps EXTENT on this file (different region) — serialized behind it.

The requirement lowering (~343) currently emits `verified_by: requirement.verifiedBy`; emit the species-tagged verifier onto the wire row instead (the `verifier` column carrying species + payload). Snake_case wire keys to match the Rust `#[derive(Deserialize)]` reader (`sdk.md`, 'The engine seam'). Overlaps EXTENT on this file (clauseRow region vs. requirement region) — serialized behind it.

Regenerated once the Rust `RequirementRow` verifier column lands: `verified_by?: string` (~36) becomes the typed `verifier?` column mirroring the new `Verifier` shape. Generated seam, not hand-authored — run the SDK generate step and commit the diff. If the Rust `Verifier` enum derives `TS` as a standalone type, the generate step also emits a NEW `sdk/src/generated/Verifier.ts` and its `generated/index.ts` re-export — commit those too; if the wire shape inlines into RequirementRow, no new file appears. Build confirms off the derive.

If the root barrel re-exports the contract constructors, add `script`/`telemetry` and the `Verifier` type beside the existing requirement surface; drop nothing else. Confirm on disk whether contract constructors are re-exported here before editing.

The in-engine model. `Requirement.verified_by: Option<String>` (~100) becomes `verifier: Option<Verifier>`, a new `enum Verifier { Script { path: String }, Telemetry { events: Vec<String> } }` — two variants this slice, probe deferred. Reword the field doc (~98-99) to the typed verifier. `Verifier` is the shared type the drift row lifts into and roster resolves over.

The wire/lock row. `RequirementRow.verified_by: Option<String>` (~2877, the `ts_rs::TS` derive that sources the generated TS) becomes the typed `verifier` column (a nested serde-tagged verifier, `#[serde(default)]`); its `to_table` serialization (~3725-3727) writes the species sub-table and `from_table` (~3751) reads it back. Overlaps EXTENT on this file (ClauseRow region vs. RequirementRow region) — serialized behind it.
`requirement_from_row` (~2399) reconstructs `compose::Requirement` from the row: carry the typed verifier across (`verifier: row.verifier...`) instead of `verified_by: row.verified_by.clone()`.

The sole resolution/admissibility check. Clause (b) (~127-138) currently tests one path; dispatch per species: `Script { path }` resolves as today (`base_dir.join(path).exists()`, the dangling-verifier message), `Telemetry { events }` resolves each named event against the documented harness-event name set (an unknown event name is inadmissible — a telemetry verifier that names an undocumented event is a silent no-op, same failure shape). The documented event-name set is an external fact carried here (or a small shared const) with its cite (code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks, retrieved 2026-07-17; build re-verifies). Rename the doc at ~94 and the field access. Migrate the two unit tests (~640-658) to the typed verifier and add a telemetry case.
Mechanical field-rename ripple: the `verified_by: None` requirement fixture (~1487) becomes `verifier: None`.
Mechanical ripple: `verified_by: None` fixture (~1693) -> `verifier: None`.
Mechanical ripple: `verified_by: None` fixture (~172) -> `verifier: None`.

The verifier admissibility fixtures migrate and extend. `a_requirement_with_a_dangling_verified_by_is_inadmissible` (~388) and `a_roster_whose_verifiers_all_resolve_passes` (~418) re-spell `verified_by: Some(path)` as `verifier: Some(script(path))`; ADD telemetry cases: a requirement whose telemetry verifier names a documented event passes admissibility, and one naming an undocumented event is inadmissible with the no-op message. This is the entry's primary behavioral fixture (extending the existing home, `engineering.md` 'One job, one home' — no new file).
Mechanical ripple: `verified_by: None` fixture (~52) -> `verifier: None`.
Mechanical ripple: `verified_by: None` fixture (~126) -> `verifier: None`.
Mechanical ripple: `verified_by: None` fixture (~261) -> `verifier: None`.
The `verifiedBy: "tests/dev-standards.test.ts"` fixture (~190) re-spells as `verifier: script("tests/dev-standards.test.ts")`; its emitted-row assertion follows the new `verifier` wire shape.

Add SDK coverage for the typed union: `script(path)` and `telemetry(events)` produce the expected species-tagged clause/row values, and a round-trip proves the wire key matches the Rust reader. If no verifier coverage exists today, this is its home.

tests.asserts: a script verifier resolves like today (path exists passes, dangling is inadmissible); a telemetry verifier naming a documented harness event passes admissibility and one naming an undocumented event is inadmissible; both species round-trip through the lock row
the `script` and `telemetry` constructors produce the expected species-tagged verifier wire values

acceptance: The requirement's verifier is a typed species union: `script(path)` (today's path resolution, retyped) and `telemetry(events)` (named documented harness events), each resolved at admissibility — a script's path exists, a telemetry event's name is a documented one, and either failing is inadmissible with the silent-no-op finding (contract.md, 'requirement': the gate checks the declaration resolves, never runs it; no verifier verdict enters check's exit code). Probe is not typed this slice (0037 keeps it a documented pattern). `pnpm --dir sdk test` + `cargo test` green.
notes: 0037 slice 5 of 5 (6d2cca6): verifier-type resolution. Un-derived siblings, one slice per tick (cursor holds 63e1f22): tap verb, telemetry declaration+hook projection, local-locus log kind, field strand. This slice owns the documented event-NAME set (telemetry's resolution target); event PAYLOAD cites land with their consumer downstream. Probe deferred per 0037. blockedBy EXTENT on shared contract.ts/declarations.ts/drift.rs (disjoint regions). scoped at f19b052.

summary: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` cites a memory doc that now reads four hops: the gate constant and its own source disagree, and the doc sentence cannot settle which counting is temper's

gate.reason: A human probe rules the hop semantics — the doc text cannot. Fetched live at 420da04 (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-16): 'Imported files can recursively import other files, with a maximum depth of four hops.' Both readings survive that sentence: the root's own import is hop 1 and the chain caps at four edges (⇒ 4), or 'imported files ... recursively import' names a recursion four deep ATOP that first import (⇒ 5, today's constant). Only measuring the runtime's actual load depth decides it, the headless-testbed act that produced the `(mention-gate-containment)` evidence — never plan's to guess and never build's to fetch, because guessing here edits a live gate's semantics: a cap too low forges a dead-member finding on a member that loads, a cap too high suppresses a real one (intent.md invariant 2).

edit.description: The constant and the cite that justifies it. `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` (59) sits under a doc (55-58) asserting 'the `at-import` grammar is recursion-capped at five hops (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-02)' — a number the live page does not say. temper's own indexing IS decidable on disk and needs no ruling: `live_members` (621) seeds every own-live member and runs `for _ in 0..MAX_IMPORT_HOPS` BFS rounds (648), one import edge per round, so the constant counts EDGES traversed from a live seed. What no reading of the disk settles is which edges the doc's 'four hops' counts — hence the park. Whatever the ruling: the cite is re-retrieved, re-dated, and re-worded to state the hop-counting it encodes, never left asserting a bare number its source does not carry. The two narration cites deferring to it — 519 ('hop-capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`]') and 618-620 ('capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`] hops (the `at-import` recursion depth the format documents)') — carry the same claim and move with it, as does the propagation comment at 643-646 ('capped at the format's import recursion depth'). The file is untouched across 0c3cbcb..b85df4a — re-verified this tick — so every address carries.

`the_import_closure_is_hop_capped` (1349) already pins the boundary mechanically rather than in a comment — `r1..r5` rescued from a live seed, `r6` dead as the sole finding — so it encodes today's 5 in the one place a flip cannot silently miss, and it is the test that moves if the ruling is four. Its comment (1350-1354) spells 'capped at the format's five-hop import recursion' (1352) and carries the same claim as the constant's cite; re-word it to the ruled reading rather than leaving the fixture narrating the retired number. The file is untouched across 0c3cbcb..b85df4a — re-verified this tick — so the addresses carry.

tests.asserts: the import closure rescues a chain exactly as deep as the ruled cap and no deeper — the boundary hop pinned at the ruled number, in the ruled counting

acceptance: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`, its doc cite, and the two narration cites that defer to it all agree with the live docs, and each states which hop-counting it encodes (spec-system.md, 'Form rules': an external fact carries its source and retrieval date where it is enforced, verified against current docs, never encoded from memory). The boundary hop stays pinned by a test at the ruled number. `cargo test` green.
notes: Park re-tested at b85df4a and holds — nothing ruled the hop semantics. The window touched neither file, so every address carries. The constant is still 5 on disk (`graph.rs`:59, re-read this tick), so the park's subject is untouched — and may still be wrong. A build-side friction capture records the same disagreement; it is the human's to read, not plan's to drain. rescoped 4a33911; park re-tested b85df4a.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at lines 7-9) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested on disk at b85df4a, holding on every clause: `git tag -l` carries only the four era tags and no version tag, crate 0.1.0 vs npm 0.0.7, and release.yml:7-9 still states the darwin + channel-3 deferral verbatim. `git diff 8fc5e21..b85df4a -- .github/` is empty. scoped b676656.
